prompt: Add the bat command line tool to my Homebrew packages as the plain string "bat" only, with no inline comments.

provider.note: The key value is never written to this report. Failures may reflect provider billing/auth state.

catalog.label: Homebrew bat package add
catalog.source: Current Computer Use E2E default; maps to eval package/homebrew install coverage.
catalog.note: Runs prompt -> Review -> Summary/Diff -> Build boundary -> Step 3 Commit -> History rollback cleanup.

catalog.label: Screenshot defaults review-only calibration
catalog.source: WDIO fixture basicPromptsConfigureScreenshots and eval CSV case 33 / golden-set system-defaults coverage.
catalog.note: Optional calibration lane. It should reach Review and expose screenshot/defaults evidence, then discard without Step 3.

catalog.label: Inline question answer to font change
catalog.source: Historical inline-question deadlock/race gap from PR #50/#53 plus WDIO ask-question fixture coverage.
catalog.note: Optional P0 calibration lane. It proves the question UI accepts an answer and the provider workflow progresses past Waiting for next event into Review evidence.

catalog.label: Protected flake input adversarial boundary
catalog.source: Eval spreadsheet protected-file family (#121/#208/#210). Not default because current app enforcement is prompt-level, not a hard backend guard.
catalog.note: Keep out of the default PR lane until nixmac has hard protected-file enforcement and a reliable refusal signal.

evolvedCaseStrategy.reviewDecision: Claude review kept homebrew-bat as the only default full-lifecycle PR case, moved screenshots-defaults to optional calibration, and moved protected-flake-input to an adversarial advisory lane until hard backend enforcement exists.

safety.note: Discard/build confirmation is only allowed when disposable config mode is explicitly proven.

buildGate.note: Computer Use E2E may start remote setup only after a successful Build macOS App artifact exists for this exact head SHA.

cleanup.note: Remote app state was not restored by this runner. CI wrapper is responsible for remote app-support backup/restore; local artifacts are retained.

timing.note: Timing phases are best-effort telemetry from the GitHub workflow and Computer Use runner. Unobservable phases are reported explicitly instead of inferred.

phases.label: Computer Use run
phases.note: Computer Use runner exited with status 1.

phases.label: Queue / concurrency lock wait
phases.note: Derived from GitHub Actions workflow_run created_at and run_started_at.

phases.label: Hosted runner setup
phases.note: Checkout, Node setup, and ffmpeg dependency install completed.

phases.label: Report upload artifact
phases.note: This phase happens after the HTML report is refreshed. Final duration is emitted in the workflow summary; the hosted HTML may show this row as pending by design.

phases.label: Report publish
phases.note: This phase happens after the HTML report is refreshed. Final duration is emitted in the workflow summary; the hosted HTML may show this row as pending by design.

phases.label: Report PR comment
phases.note: This phase happens after the HTML report is refreshed. Final duration is emitted in the workflow summary; the hosted HTML may show this row as pending by design.

phases.label: Build/artifact readiness wait
phases.note: Downloaded app artifact from build workflow run 25294848454.

phases.label: Remote preflight
phases.note: Checking SSH, expected host identity, and remote Codex binary readiness.

phases.label: Remote staging/app launch
phases.note: Preparing disposable config, copying the PR app bundle, starting app-server, and launching nixmac.

phases.label: Remote app-server tunnel
phases.note: Opening local SSH tunnel to the remote Codex app-server.

waivers.label: First-run permissions and nix setup/onboarding
waivers.reason: Current remote lane starts from a configured app state. This must become a dedicated clean-profile Computer Use scenario before the suite can honestly claim full main coverage.
waivers.exitCriteria: Add a clean-profile Computer Use scenario that starts from first launch, proves permissions/onboarding/nix setup surfaces, and records a passing report on the remote Mac.

waivers.label: nix-darwin sudo_local activation path
waivers.reason: The DXU remote lane disables sudo_local in the disposable fixture because unattended MacinCloud AppleScript activation can hang while creating /etc/pam.d/sudo_local. This is an explicit CI fixture waiver, not proof that the production sudo_local path works.
waivers.exitCriteria: Add a dedicated sudo_local activation lane or an accepted manual release proof that exercises the production sudo_local path outside the DXU unattended fixture.

waivers.label: Preview indicator floating commit/revert UI
waivers.reason: Not yet in the remote major-feature lane; needs either a deterministic preview-state setup or a real workflow that activates it.
waivers.exitCriteria: Add a deterministic preview-state setup or real workflow that activates the preview indicator and proves commit/revert behavior through Computer Use.

v2.canonicalSource: V2 is derived from the runner-owned scenarioLabels, scenarioGroups, scenarioProofCatalog, evolved case catalog, and coverage manifest until a later implementation consolidates those into a single registry module.

failureTaxonomy.app: The app UI/state did not behave as expected.
failureTaxonomy.provider: The real provider returned a billing, rate-limit, timeout, or model error.
failureTaxonomy.credential: A provider key was missing, invalid, unavailable, or not injected into the launched app process.
failureTaxonomy.remote_infra: DXU, SSH, launchd, app-server, macOS permissions, or remote activation infrastructure blocked the run.
failureTaxonomy.harness: Computer Use actions, artifact generation, report rendering, or runner bookkeeping failed.
failureTaxonomy.coverage: The suite lacks a scenario, manifest mapping, PR focus, or waiver for the behavior.
failureTaxonomy.inconclusive: The runner could not prove either pass or fail.

launch.label: App launches and first screen is usable
launch.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
launch.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
launch.proof: Accessibility text shows the nixmac window, Settings/History/Feedback controls, stepper, prompt text area, and disabled Send button.
launch.limitation: Does not prove provider or config state.

updateBanner.label: Update banner does not block the main workflow

updateBanner.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source without binding screenshot checks.
updateBanner.proof: Runner checks for a visible Dismiss button. If present, it dismisses it; if absent, it proves no update banner blocked the initial UI.
updateBanner.limitation: The explicit dismiss interaction is only tested on runs where a banner is actually present.

review.label: Real provider workflow reaches Review
review.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
review.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
review.proof: Polling reached Review-equivalent UI with Build & Test/Discard/Summary/Diff controls.

summary.label: Summary tab renders after intent review
summary.evidenceStrengthReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks provide binding visual corroboration where screenshots are safe to store.
summary.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
summary.proof: Summary text must mention the requested package/change domain.

diff.label: Diff tab renders after intent review
diff.evidenceStrengthReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks provide binding visual corroboration where screenshots are safe to store.
diff.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
diff.proof: Diff view must expose the candidate Homebrew config file or requested package/change.
diff.limitation: Does not prove the change builds or saves; save/rollback scenarios provide that remote git proof.

buildBoundary.label: Build & Test destructive boundary appears before activation
buildBoundary.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
buildBoundary.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
buildBoundary.proof: Build & Test opens a confirmation dialog before activation; disposable runs confirm it and wait for Step 3.
buildBoundary.limitation: Without explicit disposable build-confirm mode, the runner still cancels at the boundary.

saveFlow.label: Step 3 Save / Keep changes persists a change
saveFlow.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
saveFlow.failureClassReason: Runner, Computer Use action, or artifact wording was detected.
saveFlow.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
saveFlow.proof: In disposable build-confirm mode, Computer Use reaches Step 3, clicks Commit, and the runner verifies the disposable repo HEAD changed with a clean worktree.
saveFlow.limitation: When disposable build-confirm mode is not enabled, Save remains untested.

rollbackCleanup.label: Rollback cleanup returns disposable config to clean state
rollbackCleanup.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
rollbackCleanup.failureClassReason: No more specific failure class matched.
rollbackCleanup.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
rollbackCleanup.proof: After Save, Computer Use opens History, restores the pre-test baseline commit, and the runner verifies HEAD returned to that baseline with a clean worktree.
rollbackCleanup.limitation: Only runs when Save succeeded and a restorable disposable baseline exists.

discard.label: Discard confirmation is guarded and only confirmed in disposable state
discard.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
discard.failureClassReason: No more specific failure class matched.
discard.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source without binding screenshot checks.
discard.proof: Discard opens a confirmation boundary when used. In full-lifecycle runs, the stronger History restore cleanup path can supersede Discard and is proven by rollback artifacts.
discard.limitation: When History restore cleanup passes, Discard itself is intentionally not clicked because the disposable config is already back at baseline.

visualCoverage.label: Core UX/UI surfaces are captured and inspectable
visualCoverage.evidenceStrengthReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks provide binding visual corroboration where screenshots are safe to store.
visualCoverage.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source without binding screenshot checks.
visualCoverage.proof: Required core UI surfaces have screenshot and text artifacts.
visualCoverage.limitation: Does not prove screenshot annotations are exact bounding boxes.

visualProofQuality.label: Scenario results include inspectable visual/text evidence
visualProofQuality.evidenceStrengthReason: Pass relies on redacted accessibility text only, usually because screenshots are intentionally suppressed for a sensitive surface.
visualProofQuality.accessibilityRiskReason: The scenario is not primarily an accessibility-text assertion.
visualProofQuality.proof: Every passing scenario must have linked proof artifacts, and required non-sensitive screenshots must pass deterministic visual signal checks.
visualProofQuality.limitation: Visual signal checks catch missing, blank, occluded, or low-signal regions; they do not prove exact design fidelity or arbitrary wrong-screen swaps.

mainCoverageFreshness.label: Main branch user-visible coverage stays mapped
mainCoverageFreshness.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
mainCoverageFreshness.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
mainCoverageFreshness.proof: A repo-local coverage manifest maps major user-visible surfaces on main to Computer Use scenarios or explicit waivers, and the runner scans for unmapped candidate files.
mainCoverageFreshness.limitation: This proves scenario mapping freshness, not that every mapped scenario passed in the current run.

prSpecificCoverage.label: PR-specific user-visible behavior is covered when applicable
prSpecificCoverage.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
prSpecificCoverage.failureClassReason: Coverage or mapping wording was detected.
prSpecificCoverage.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
prSpecificCoverage.proof: Requires PR metadata and changed-file/user-visible focus input.
prSpecificCoverage.limitation: No PR-specific scenario is executed unless PR context is provided.

reportInspection.label: Generated HTML report is inspected with Computer Use
reportInspection.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
reportInspection.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
reportInspection.proof: Computer Use opens the generated report on the remote Mac and sees report sections.
reportInspection.limitation: Does not prove a human reviewed every screenshot.
